title: Express 5 Routing, Path Syntax, Parameters, and Dispatch · Type-System Trace: Route Order Shadowing
summary: An Express 4 route table is moved unchanged, wildcard values change shape, optional syntax breaks, route order shadows literals, and decoded parameters are trusted as identifiers. Planned artifact: Add and verify the httpsts route order shadowing increment in Express 5 route grammar and dispatch table. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Derive dispatch from registration order, method, path, router mounting, next-route behavior, and literal-versus-parameter overlap.
Author Express 5.2 string paths with named wildcards, braces, reserved characters, and supported path-to-regexp syntax.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Node and Express server Predict Route Order Shadowing · Theory Route Order Shadowing
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory team handles case 2267 in a public incident API behind one regional edge proxy. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to preserve Express 5 route order and named wildcard-array evidence; retain compatibility with one older generated client; then fail the canary while the old revision drains and prove rollback remains safe. Browser code models pure decisions only. Node, Express, listener, TLS, PostgreSQL, proxy, worker, load, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Route Order Shadowing contract for the supplied data.

Evidence target: Node and Express server Predict Route Order Shadowing · Theory Route Order Shadowing.
